en.extract:
The imitator goshawk or imitator sparrowhawk (Tachyspiza imitator) is a species of bird of prey in the family Accipitridae. It was formerly placed in the genus Accipiter. It is found on the islands of Bougainville, Choiseul and Santa Isabel in the Solomon Islands archipelago. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest and subtropical or tropical moist montane forest. 
It is threatened by habitat loss.


Description
It has a length of 26-33 cm and a wingspan of 53-63 cm. Information on mass is very limited, but one male weighed 208 grams and two females weighed 225 and 250 grams, respectively.


References

BirdLife Species Factsheet.

The '''imitator goshawk''' or '''imitator sparrowhawk''' ('''''Tachyspiza imitator''''') is a species of [[bird of prey]] in the family [[Accipitridae]]. It was formerly placed in the [[genus]] ''[[Accipiter]]''. It is found on the islands of [[Bougainville Island|Bougainville]], [[Choiseul Island|Choiseul]] and [[Santa Isabel Island|Santa Isabel]] in the [[Solomon Islands (archipelago)|Solomon Islands archipelago]]. Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]] and subtropical or tropical moist [[montane forest]]. 

It is threatened by [[habitat loss]].

== Description ==
It has a length of 26-33 cm and a wingspan of 53-63 cm.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Ferguson-Lees |first=James |title=Raptors of the World |date=2005 |publisher=Bloomsbury Publishing Plc |others=David A. Christie |isbn=978-0-7136-6957-2 |series=Helm Field Guides |location=London}}</ref> Information on mass is very limited, but one male weighed 208 grams and two females weighed 225 and 250 grams, respectively.<ref>{{Cite book |last=Dunning Jr. |first=John B. |url=https://www.taylorfrancis.com/books/9781420064452 |title=CRC Handbook of Avian Body Masses |date=2007-12-05 |publisher=CRC Press |isbn=978-0-429-14982-5 |edition=0 |language=en |doi=10.1201/9781420064452}}</ref>
